_comment: Deterministic pattern library for Git Gud Security scan.py. grep/config tier only. IDs match references/checks.md. Each finding is a CANDIDATE the skill confirms at file:line before reporting. 'any' = report if any regex matches a line (case-insensitive). 'not' = suppress that line if it also matches. 'secret':true redacts the match in output. kind 'filename' flags a file's presence.

patterns.title: Hardcoded API key / token in source
patterns.fix: Pull every credential from an env var or secret manager at runtime; remove the literal and rotate the leaked key.

patterns.title: Private key embedded in source
patterns.fix: Never commit private keys. Remove, rotate, and load from a secret store or mounted file at runtime.

patterns.title: Private secret wrapped in a client-exposed env prefix
patterns.fix: Public-prefixed vars are inlined into the client bundle. Move the real secret to a non-prefixed server var and proxy the call through a server route.

patterns.title: Supabase service_role key referenced in app code
patterns.fix: service_role bypasses all RLS. Use the anon/publishable key client-side; keep service_role only in server routes / edge functions.

patterns.title: Database connection string with inline password
patterns.fix: Move connection strings to env/secret manager and rotate the password; never commit live DB creds.

patterns.title: Secret-looking literal assigned in source
patterns.fix: Read it from an env var; if the value is real, rotate it. Confirm this isn't a placeholder before reporting.

patterns.title: JWT signing secret hardcoded
patterns.fix: Anyone with this secret can forge any token. Move to env/secret store and rotate; invalidate existing tokens.

patterns.title: Key / credential file committed to the repo
patterns.fix: Remove from the tree, add to .gitignore, rotate the credential, and purge from git history.

patterns.title: Terraform state committed (often holds plaintext secrets)
patterns.fix: Never commit tfstate. Use a remote backend (S3+DynamoDB, TF Cloud), gitignore it, and rotate any secrets it exposed.

patterns.title: RLS disabled on a table (public API fully open)
patterns.fix: Enable RLS on every table exposed via the anon key and add an explicit owner-scoped policy.

patterns.title: RLS policy with a permanently-true condition
patterns.fix: A policy of using(true) is the same as no policy. Scope to auth.uid() = owner_id (or your tenant column).

patterns.title: Firebase security rule allows anyone to read/write
patterns.fix: Replace `if true` with `if request.auth != null && request.auth.uid == resource.data.ownerId` (scope to the owner).

patterns.title: Firebase test-mode rules shipped (time-bomb open access)
patterns.fix: Test-mode rules open the DB until a date then lock it. Replace with real auth/ownership rules before launch.

patterns.title: Serverless / edge function with no auth check
patterns.fix: Require and verify the caller's JWT (or a service token) at the top of the function; don't trust the client.

patterns.title: eval() of dynamic input
patterns.fix: eval runs arbitrary code. Use JSON.parse for data, ast.literal_eval for Python literals, or a safe expression parser.

patterns.title: new Function() with dynamic body
patterns.fix: Interpolating untrusted strings into new Function() is code injection. Use a safe lookup/parser instead.

patterns.title: Shell exec with interpolated input
patterns.fix: Use execFile/spawn with an argument array; never build a shell string from user input.

patterns.title: os.system / subprocess shell=True
patterns.fix: Pass args as a list without shell=True so shell metacharacters can't be interpreted.

patterns.title: SQL built by string interpolation
patterns.fix: Use parameterized queries / bound params; never concatenate user input into SQL.

patterns.title: yaml.load without SafeLoader
patterns.fix: Use yaml.safe_load(); yaml.load() executes arbitrary Python via tags.

patterns.title: Unsafe deserialization of untrusted data
patterns.fix: Prefer JSON / a schema-validated deserializer; never unpickle data from an untrusted source.

patterns.title: TLS certificate verification disabled
patterns.fix: Don't disable TLS verification (enables MITM). Trust the proper CA instead.

patterns.title: dangerouslySetInnerHTML / innerHTML XSS sink
patterns.fix: Use textContent or sanitize with DOMPurify before inserting HTML.

patterns.title: Server-side template rendered from user input
patterns.fix: Render fixed templates and pass user data as bound variables; never compile templates from user input.

patterns.title: JWT decoded without verifying signature
patterns.fix: Use jwt.verify with a fixed algorithm allowlist and the secret/public key; a decoded-but-unverified token is attacker-controlled.

patterns.title: JWT alg 'none' or unrestricted algorithms accepted
patterns.fix: Pin a single expected algorithm (e.g. RS256/HS256); reject 'none' and never let the token choose.

patterns.title: Admin / auth gate compared against a hardcoded value
patterns.fix: Verify auth against a server-side store with hashed comparison; don't gate on a guessable constant.

patterns.title: CORS allows any origin (with or without credentials)
patterns.fix: Echo an explicit allowlist of trusted origins; never combine `*` (or reflected origin) with credentials.

patterns.title: Session cookie missing HttpOnly / Secure
patterns.fix: Set HttpOnly, Secure, and SameSite=Lax/Strict on auth/session cookies.

patterns.title: Debug mode enabled
patterns.fix: Disable debug in production; debug pages leak stack traces, env, and sometimes a code-exec console.

patterns.title: Source maps present in build output
patterns.fix: Don't ship .map files to production; they reverse your minified bundle (and sometimes leak comments/paths).

patterns.title: Untrusted GitHub event field interpolated into run:
patterns.fix: Pass the field through an env: var and reference $VAR in run:, never inline ${{ github.event... }}.

patterns.title: pull_request_target builds untrusted PR code
patterns.fix: pull_request_target runs with secrets. Don't checkout/build/run the PR head under it; split trusted/untrusted jobs.

patterns.title: Workflow grants write-all / broad token permissions
patterns.fix: Set least-privilege `permissions:` per job; default to contents: read.

patterns.title: Third-party GitHub Action pinned to a mutable ref
patterns.fix: Pin third-party actions to a full commit SHA; a mutable tag/branch can be moved to malicious code.

patterns.title: curl | sh remote-execution install step
patterns.fix: Download, inspect, and pin (checksum) before executing; piping a remote script to a shell runs whatever the server sends.

patterns.title: package.json postinstall runs remote / opaque code
patterns.fix: Remove network/remote-exec from install scripts; review any dependency that needs them.

patterns.title: Slash command / agent grants unrestricted tools
patterns.fix: Scope allowed-tools to the exact commands needed (e.g. Bash(git status:*)); a wildcard lets the command run anything.

patterns.title: Hook reads secrets/dotfiles and sends them out
patterns.fix: A hook that reads env/SSH/.aws and POSTs to a remote is exfiltration. Remove it and rotate anything it could have read.

patterns.title: Hook writes execution-hijacking env via $CLAUDE_ENV_FILE
patterns.fix: Treat $CLAUDE_ENV_FILE writes as privileged; a hook exporting NODE_OPTIONS/LD_PRELOAD/*_BASE_URL hijacks every later process. Remove it.

patterns.title: Hook / skill fetches remote code and executes it
patterns.fix: Never fetch-and-run remote code from a hook/skill; vendor and pin the script, or drop it.

patterns.title: settings.json widens permissions / auto-approves dangerous tools
patterns.fix: Don't ship bypassPermissions or Bash(*) allows; grant the narrowest permission set the project needs.

patterns.title: Hidden / override instructions in a skill, agent, or command file
patterns.fix: Instruction files are read as trusted context. Remove embedded directives that override the user or hide actions.

patterns.title: Zero-width / bidi unicode in an instruction file
patterns.fix: Invisible/bidi characters can hide injected instructions. Strip them; instruction text should be plain visible ASCII/UTF-8.

patterns.title: MCP config / plugin points at an unpinned remote server
patterns.fix: Pin MCP server packages to an exact version and trust the source; an unpinned/remote server can change under you.

patterns.title: Secret hardcoded in MCP / plugin config
patterns.fix: Reference secrets via ${ENV_VAR}; don't paste live keys into committed MCP config.

patterns.title: MCP server listens on the network with no auth
patterns.fix: Bind stdio/loopback or require an auth token; an MCP server on 0.0.0.0 with no auth exposes every tool to the network.

patterns.title: MCP tool passes its argument into a shell / eval
patterns.fix: Validate/allowlist tool params and use argument-array exec; tool inputs are model/attacker-controlled.

patterns.title: Electron webPreferences disable the security model
patterns.fix: Keep contextIsolation:true, nodeIntegration:false, webSecurity:true; expose only vetted APIs through a preload contextBridge.

patterns.title: Browser extension requests all-URLs host permissions
patterns.fix: Scope host_permissions and content-script matches to the exact origins needed; <all_urls> lets one compromise touch every site.

patterns.title: Extension exposes web_accessible_resources to all sites
patterns.fix: List specific resources and restrict matches to your own origins; a broad web_accessible_resources enables fingerprinting and abuse.

patterns.title: Tauri allowlist grants broad fs/shell access
patterns.fix: Enable only the Tauri APIs actually used and scope fs/shell to required paths; 'all: true' hands the webview full host power.

patterns.title: Password hashed with a fast/broken algorithm
patterns.fix: Hash passwords with bcrypt, scrypt, or argon2id (salted, slow). MD5/SHA1/unsalted SHA-256 are crackable at scale.

patterns.title: AES-ECB or unauthenticated cipher mode
patterns.fix: Use AES-GCM (authenticated) or AES-CBC+HMAC; ECB leaks plaintext structure (identical blocks encrypt identically).

patterns.title: Math.random() used to mint a token/id
patterns.fix: Math.random() is predictable. Use crypto.randomUUID() / crypto.getRandomValues() / randomBytes for anything security-relevant.

patterns.title: Auth token stored in localStorage / AsyncStorage
patterns.fix: Web: prefer httpOnly Secure cookies (localStorage is XSS-readable). Mobile: use Keychain/Keystore via expo-secure-store, not AsyncStorage.

patterns.title: Mobile app allows cleartext (HTTP) traffic
patterns.fix: Disable cleartext: usesCleartextTraffic=false with a locked network-security-config; NSAllowsArbitraryLoads=false. Cleartext enables MITM.

patterns.title: Android app shipped debuggable
patterns.fix: Never ship android:debuggable=true; it lets anyone attach a debugger and read app memory and data on a real device.

patterns.title: Android allowBackup enabled (data extractable via adb)
patterns.fix: Set android:allowBackup=false (plus backup rules) for apps holding tokens/PII; allowBackup lets `adb backup` pull app data.
